purpose: Language-specific dangerous code patterns for the code-reviewer skill. Each entry maps to a severity level and a safe alternative.

critical.description: Executes arbitrary Python code from a string. If user input reaches this, it's Remote Code Execution.
critical.safe_alternative: Use ast.literal_eval() for parsing literals, or redesign to avoid dynamic execution.

critical.description: Same risk as eval() — arbitrary code execution.
critical.safe_alternative: Restructure code to avoid dynamic execution. Consider a whitelist-based dispatch pattern.

critical.description: Deserializes arbitrary Python objects. Malicious pickle data executes code on load.
critical.safe_alternative: Use json.loads() for data exchange. If pickle is required internally, never deserialize data from untrusted sources.

critical.description: Same risk as pickle — arbitrary code execution on load.
critical.safe_alternative: Use JSON or MessagePack for serialization.

critical.description: Passes the command to the shell interpreter. User input in the command → shell injection.
critical.safe_alternative: Pass command as a list: subprocess.run(['ls', '-la', path]) — never with shell=True when any part comes from user input.

critical.description: Runs a shell command. Any user-controlled input → shell injection.
critical.safe_alternative: Use subprocess.run() with a list argument and shell=False.

critical.description: Default YAML loader can instantiate arbitrary Python objects via !!python/object tags.
critical.safe_alternative: Always use yaml.safe_load() for untrusted input.

high.description: Dynamic imports can be abused to load arbitrary modules if user controls the argument.
high.safe_alternative: Use explicit static imports. If dynamic loading is needed, use importlib with a strict module allowlist.

high.description: User-supplied path passed to open() — path traversal risk.
high.safe_alternative: Resolve path with os.path.realpath() and verify it starts with an expected base directory.

high.description: Disables SSL certificate verification — enables man-in-the-middle attacks.
high.safe_alternative: Remove verify=False. If using a self-signed cert, pass verify='/path/to/cert.pem'.

high.description: Python's random module is not cryptographically secure. Do not use for tokens, nonces, or secrets.
high.safe_alternative: Use secrets.token_urlsafe() or secrets.token_hex() for security-sensitive randomness.

high.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken — do not use for passwords or integrity verification.
high.safe_alternative: Use hashlib.sha256() or hashlib.sha3_256() for integrity. Use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords.

high.description: SHA-1 is collision-vulnerable and deprecated for security use.
high.safe_alternative: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 for hashing. Use bcrypt/argon2/scrypt for passwords.

medium.description: Silently swallows all exceptions — hides bugs and prevents proper error handling.
medium.safe_alternative: Catch specific exceptions. Log unexpected errors. Never use bare pass in security-sensitive paths.

medium.description: assert statements are removed when Python runs with -O flag. Never use for security checks.
medium.safe_alternative: Use explicit if/raise for security-relevant validation.

medium.description: Logging passwords or tokens to stdout leaks sensitive data.
medium.safe_alternative: Redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with explicit field exclusions.

critical.description: Executes arbitrary JavaScript from a string. If user input reaches this, it's XSS or RCE.
critical.safe_alternative: Remove eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, explicit function dispatch for logic.

critical.description: Equivalent to eval() — executes arbitrary code from a string.
critical.safe_alternative: Eliminate dynamic code construction. Use static functions or a dispatch table.

critical.description: Setting innerHTML with user-controlled data enables DOM-based XSS.
critical.safe_alternative: Use textContent for plain text. Use DOMPurify.sanitize() if HTML is required.

critical.description: React's escape hatch for raw HTML — dangerous if content is user-supplied.
critical.safe_alternative: Use a sanitization library like DOMPurify before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

critical.description: Executes shell commands. User input in the command → shell injection.
critical.safe_alternative: Use child_process.execFile() or spawn() with argument arrays, never shell string construction.

high.description: Can inject arbitrary HTML/script content — XSS risk.
high.safe_alternative: Use DOM manipulation methods (createElement, appendChild) instead.

high.description: Open redirect — user-controlled URL in redirect enables phishing.
high.safe_alternative: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist of permitted domains.

high.description: Not cryptographically secure. Do not use for tokens, session IDs, or nonces.
Merging user objects without hasOwnProperty check can pollute Object.prototype.
high.safe_alternative: Use Object.assign with null prototype objects. Validate keys before merge. Use lodash's _.merge() carefully.

medium.safe_alternative: Log errors with context. Re-throw or handle appropriately.

medium.description: May log passwords, tokens, or PII from request bodies.
medium.safe_alternative: Redact sensitive fields before logging. Use a structured logger with field-level sanitization.

critical.description: Building SQL queries with + or string formatting from user input enables SQL injection.
critical.safe_alternative: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s', (user_id,))

critical.pattern: dynamic ORDER BY from user input
critical.description: Column name used in ORDER BY cannot be parameterized — requires explicit allowlist.
critical.safe_alternative: Map user input to a hardcoded column name allowlist: allowed = {'name': 'name', 'date': 'created_at'}; col = allowed.get(user_input, 'name')

high.description: Bypasses Go's type safety. Memory corruption risk if misused.
high.safe_alternative: Avoid unsafe package unless performance-critical and extensively tested. Document every use.

high.description: User input in shell command → command injection.
high.safe_alternative: Pass arguments as separate strings to exec.Command, never concatenate into a shell string.

critical.description: Java deserialization of untrusted data can execute arbitrary code (gadget chains).
critical.safe_alternative: Use JSON/XML serialization. If Java serialization is required, implement a deserialization filter (ObjectInputFilter).

critical.description: OS command execution — user input → command injection.
critical.safe_alternative: Use ProcessBuilder with a list of arguments. Never construct command strings from user input.

high.safe_alternative: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3. Use BCrypt for passwords.

high.description: Using Statement instead of PreparedStatement with user input → SQL injection.

hardcoded_secrets.description: Hardcoded credentials in source code are exposed in version control and binaries.
hardcoded_secrets.safe_alternative: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, Azure Key Vault).

missing_input_validation.description: User input used directly in operations without length, type, or format validation.
missing_input_validation.safe_alternative: Validate all inputs at system boundaries: check type, length, format, and range before use.
